title: I Removed 48 Years Of Toxins From My Blood

chunks.title: Introduction to Blood Cleaning Procedure
chunks.summary: The speaker introduces the video, explaining their trip to Spain for a blood cleaning procedure known as inuspheresis. They detail how this procedure works, specifically its ability to filter out toxins such as heavy metals and microplastics from the blood.
chunks.transcript: What's up, guys? I came all the way to Spain to get my blood cleaned. [music] [music] In this video, I'm going to show you all about this blood cleaning procedure that I traveled halfway around the world to do. I got on a flight to Newark. I got on a flight from Newark to Morca. I got into Mayor Orca last night. I'm jetlagged. I already swam in the ocean. Tomorrow I'm going to get a blood cleaning procedure called anus feresis. Not to be confused with anus feresis. It's inus feresis. It's nothing with my anus. It's like tpe, but you don't have to worry about getting donor plasma. Come on over here, guys. You got to see this view where we're staying in my orca. This is crazy, right? So, while we're here, I'm going to be swimming in the Mediterranean recovering. But here's the deal. Therapeutic plasma exchange, TPE. You guys may have seen Brian Johnson do this. He got a plasma transfusion from his son and then he gave his plasma to his dad. The thing that's interesting to me about inuspheresis is that it doesn't actually have to do an exchange of plasma. It's just a very small filter like a tenonometer filter that's going to filter my blood. It takes out inflammatory proteins, takes out heavy metals, takes out microplastics, PFAS, volatile organic compounds. It's one of the only things I think that we know of that takes out microplastics from your blood. I'm going to take you guys through the whole procedure tomorrow when I get my first anuspheresis blood cleaning. Then I'm going to have a day off and then I'm going to do a second one and you're actually going to be able to see what comes out of my blood. It's pretty crazy the photos I've seen. Then we're going to test it. So I tested all my blood work before I came to Spain and then I'm going to test all my blood work again. I'm going to show you all the pre and post results. inuspheresis isn't available in the states right now, but it's going to be available in 2026. And I really feel like this is going to become a commonplace health longevity treatment. There's just so many toxins that we're exposed to in our environment that you can't get rid of that are hard. PFAs, forever chemicals, these things take a long time to get out of our bodies. Microplastics, there's not a great way to detox these. So, I'm curious, am I going to feel better, nothing, or worse after this blood cleaning procedure? In case you guys didn't know, I'm 48 years old. I've had a lifetime of poor decisions. The last 10, 15 years of my life, I've been eating super freaking clean, as clean as I possibly can. But nobody's perfect, right? And I had a lifetime of exposure to microplastics, all these things. And I want to live a long, healthy life. I want to stay vital and verl. And so I'm curious about how this blood cleaning procedure is going to work. I'm going to go swim in the Mediterranean right now and chill out for the rest of today.

chunks.title: Presentation of Findings
chunks.summary: The speaker reveals the results from the blood analysis post-treatment, noting a surprisingly high presence of pesticides while heavy metals remained low. They share reflections on their diet and past exposures to these toxins.
chunks.transcript: cleaning procedure. All right, guys. I am back in Costa Rica. I have all of the results from my inispheresis procedure in my orca and I want to share them with you. It's a little technical and I was really floored at what was in my body. So, I'll say from the outset that my metals were very low. I didn't have a lot of heavy metals which I expected, but I had a lot of pesticides. So, take a look at this. I'm going to show you my actual analysis. I did the most comprehensive analysis. This cost me thousands of dollars to share with you guys this information, but you can see here that I had four the four highest analytes that were in my sample. And I'll show you guys again here a picture of my inispheresis eluate. So that that dark stuff at the bottom, that brown stuff at the bottom, this is all in there. So this is not urine, right? This is filtrate from the plasma. So in my blood were very high levels of multiple pesticides and plastic chemicals. There was very high levels of DDE in my blood which is not great. Now DDE is a degradation product of DDT. It's fat soluble. So it's accumulating in fats soluble foods that I'm eating and it's residue from pesticides that I've eaten over the course of my 48 years. One of the broad questions that I have to answer is is this recent exposure or is this 48 years of accumulated toxins and pul? Thankfully, most of this is out of my system. Remember, I did two of these inpheresis procedures. And this is all just the LU8 from the first procedure. What I will do is in a year, I will go back and do insperesis. I'll do another video and I'll share with you guys what's in the eluate from that. So, I'll know whether this is from 48 years of accumulated toxins or I'm currently being exposed to these things. I eat mostly organic here in Costa Rica and in the States. I do everything I can. I'm in the ocean surfing every day, but I don't think that's a major exposure. It's a huge dilution. I don't have huge exposures that I can think of. But what causes cancer? Why do humans get cancer? Why do humans get dementia? Well, it's insulin resistance, but it's compounds like this that we're never able to get out of our bodies. This is why I think this is such an interesting procedure. I don't love biohacking, but if I can detoxify 48 years of accumulated toxin load in my body and change the course of my health in a way that I wouldn't be able to do with the cleanest diet that I possibly can, that's amazing. and it's really cool. I also had gamma hexacchloro cycllohexane which is known as lindane. It's another insecticide pesticide drinking water. All my drinking water in Costa Rica is reverse osmosis. I'm not getting it there. I had dicchlorommethane solvents, paints, varnishes, plastic industry adhesives and sprays. Trimethylenzene solvents, medicines, perfumes and paints. chunks.title: Conclusions and Future Steps
chunks.summary: In closing, the speaker reflects on the significance of the inuspheresis procedure for their health and plans to repeat it annually. They emphasize the importance of addressing accumulated toxins and compare the procedure to other health interventions.
